title: limonium delicatulum subsp. latebracteatum
intro: Limonium delicatulum subsp. latebracteatum is a subspecies of the daisy-like plant Limonium delicatulum, which is commonly known as delicate sea lavender. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The health benefits of Limonium delicatulum subsp. latebracteatum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
